title: Running Uniface Services on the iSeries
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
Running Uniface Services on the iSeries
text: Running Uniface Services on the iSeries To run a service, the Uniface Server must be able to find the UAR containing the .svc file and its signature. Compile the service, its signature, and any object it needs into an archive for use by the Uniface Server ( USERVER ). To compile objects directly to the iSeries, set $resources_output in the client assignment file so that it points to an archive on your iSeries machine. For example: [PATHS] $AS4 = TCP: Machinename +13001| User | Password [SETTINGS] $resources_output = $AS4:my_svc.uar Copy Alternatively, you can transfer a UAR in binarymode to the iSeries. In the client assignment file, redirect service activation to the iSeries Uniface Router ( UROUTER ). For example: [PATHS] $AS4 = TCP: Machinename +13001| User | Password [SERVICES_EXEC] A* $AS4:A* Copy In this example, activation by the client of any service whose name starts with an ‘A’ will be routed to an ANY USERVER on the iSeries. For more information, see Assignments for Remote Services and Reports . Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
